title: Rustic Three-Drawer Bathroom Vanity
summary: A floor-standing vanity with edge-glued 1x6 side panels, a pocket-holed face frame, and three real drawers on euro slides — sized to build your own top around your own sink.
description:
The sides here are three 1x6 boards glued edge to edge on a flat surface rather than a plywood panel, giving the vanity a genuine board-and-batten look once the 1x2 trim caps the top. Build the front face frame as its own flat assembly, then nail and glue it to the front, checking for square by comparing diagonal measurements before moving on.

**The interior back pieces aren't just a finished surface — they're what the drawer slides actually attach to**, while still hiding the plywood-free construction so no raw edges show from behind. Build full-size drawers with pocket holes where there's no plumbing to work around; where a drawer has to clear plumbing, keep the same basic cuts but box out the corners and set the back where it needs to pass the pipe.

**The top is intentionally open-ended — build it to suit your own sink**, using 1x boards pocket-holed together with a cutout sized to your actual sink rather than a fixed opening. Drawer slides mount to the front 1x2 cleat and a rear end socket; follow your hardware's own instructions for spacing.

tools.note: For cutting the sink opening in the top.

materials.name: Euro style drawer slides, 16 in, pair
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the side panels
steps.body: Glue three 1x6 boards edge to edge on a flat surface for each side. Attach the 1x2 trim on top, overhanging the ends by 3/4", leaving a 2-1/2" space under the bottom trim.

steps.title: Attach the back supports
steps.body: Drill two pocket holes on each end of the back support boards and attach with pocket hole screws, exposed on the back.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame flat with pocket holes and pocket hole screws, then nail and glue to the front of the cabinet. Check for square with diagonal measurements.

steps.title: Attach the back panels
steps.body: Nail and glue the back panel boards to the sides and top/bottom supports, giving the drawer slides something solid to attach to.

steps.title: Finish the front trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the front trim to fit, then nail and glue in place.

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Build the drawer boxes with pocket holes, boxing out corners around plumbing where needed. Nail and glue the plywood bottoms on.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Attach the drawer slides to the front 1x2 cleat and a rear end socket, following the hardware's instructions.
materials: Euro style drawer slides, 16 in, pair

steps.title: Build the top
steps.body: Build the top from 1x boards pocket-holed together, leaving an opening sized to your own sink.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces, apply primer and paint or stain and poly, and add the bin pulls.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
